document.title: Post Market Surveillance report For Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector HAD1717MC, HAD1717MG

sourceSummary.oneLine: Post Market Surveillance report A-PMS-002 Rev.0 for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents sales experience, customer feedback including complaints, similar product incident/adverse event searches, and incident/adverse/recall feedback for post-market surveillance.
keyPoints: Document No. A-PMS-002 with Revision Date Sep/12/2019 and Revision No. 0.
Product scope is Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models HAD1717MC and HAD1717MG.
Revision history states Rev. 0, Rev. date Sep/12/2019, Revision contents Establishment.
Sales experience table lists 2015 through 2019 quantities distributed as 0 for Domestic (Korea), Overseas EU, and Overseas non-EU.
Similar product incident and adverse event search sites include MDITAC, MHRA Medical Device Alerts, FDA Recalls, and FDA MAUDE.

The serious adverse event feedback table includes categories such as death, life threatening illness or injury, permanent impairment, hospitalization, medical or surgical intervention, fetal distress/death or birth defect, and other side effect.

evidence.evidenceText: 1 led to a death 2 results in a life threatening illness or injury 3 results in a permanent impairment of a body structure or body function 4 requires in patient hospitalisation or prolongation of existing hospitalisation 5 results in medical or surgical intervention to prevent permanent impairment to body structure or a body function 6 led to foetal distress, foetal death or a congenital abnormality/birth defect 7 Other side effect

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is ambiguous: source metadata authoritySignal says FDA, but the document text is a company PMS report and lists multiple search sites including MDITAC, MHRA, FDA Recalls, and FDA MAUDE.
Approval date March/16/2019 appears earlier than the prepared date Sep/12/2019, review date Sep/13/2019, and revision establishment date Sep/12/2019.
No standards are stated in the source text.
Several PMS tables are blank or contain only zeros, so incident, complaint, corrective action, notification, and recall counts are not explicitly populated beyond the displayed table structure.
